title: Can't delete certain triples (with literals) in Version 7.1.0
description:
I have noticed the following problem concerning Virtuoso through our integration tests:
Certain Triples don't get properly deleted with a normal DELETE/DELETE WHERE call on Version 7.1 (I used the docker container "encoflife/virtuoso:7.1.0" to reproduce the error)
Minimal Example for reproducing the Error in the conductor:
Insert into Default Graph URI "http://example.org/" the following Triples:
INSERT {
    <http://example.org/renameTest/old> <http://example.org/renameTest/p2> <http://example.org/renameTest/old> .
    <http://example.org/renameTest/old> <http://example.org/renameTest/p2> "http://example.org/renameTest/old" .
    <http://example.org/renameTest/old> <http://example.org/renameTest/type> "TYPE"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string> .
}

result: 3 added
Afterwards, attempt to delete them with:
DELETE {
http://example.org/renameTest/old http://example.org/renameTest/p2 http://example.org/renameTest/old .
http://example.org/renameTest/old http://example.org/renameTest/p2 "http://example.org/renameTest/old" .
http://example.org/renameTest/old http://example.org/renameTest/type "TYPE"^^http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string .
}
result: 3 deleted
Afterwards, checking the Graph with a normal Select Query:
SELECT * { ?s ?p ?o }
result:
<http://example.org/renameTest/old> <http://example.org/renameTest/p2> "http://example.org/renameTest/old" .
<http://example.org/renameTest/old> <http://example.org/renameTest/type> "TYPE"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string> .

The only way I was able to delete those are with DELETE WHERE and not specifying the Object part.
E.G.:
DELETE WHERE  {
    <http://example.org/renameTest/old> <http://example.org/renameTest/p2> ?o .
 }
key_bug_features: DELETE command fails for certain triples with literals
